title: redaction_result_{document_id}.json -- PII redaction record, one file per document v0.2
description: Record of what was redacted from one document's OCR'd text before it entered the processed layer. Produced by document-pipeline's checkpoint 2 through tools/redact_document.py, which redacts through the tools/redaction.py Redactor abstraction (today an LLM provider that only IDENTIFIES PII spans, which are then substituted deterministically in the source so non-PII is preserved by construction; a dedicated de-identification model such as OpenMed NER can drop in -- see open-decisions.md #1) -- one file per document. A possible PII leak (structured PII surviving in the output, or a model-named value not present verbatim in the source) hard-fails the document and this file is never written; over-redaction risk sets review_required true.

method.description: Redactor method label, e.g. 'llm_span_redaction' (the current LlmRedactor: LLM identifies PII spans, substitution is deterministic) or 'openmed_ner' once a dedicated de-identification model is adopted

items_redacted.description: Count of PII items redacted from this document's text (names, resident registration numbers, etc.).

categories.description: Kinds of PII found. e.g. person_name, resident_registration_number, phone_number, address

filename_pii_flagged.description: True if the ORIGINAL filename (before intake renamed it to a document_id-based name) appeared to contain PII. Content redaction alone doesn't fix a leaking filename -- intake's rename to DOC_XXX.<ext> is what actually prevents this; this field is a diagnostic flag, not a fix applied here.
